title: Spice Girls and EoE
wikitext: []: Spice Girls & EoE             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Mon Feb 12, 2024 5:31 pm For inexplicable reasons, the hit 90s pop song "Wannabe" by the Spice Girls popped in my head while I was reading a 15 year old analysis about the pre-3rd instrumentality Shinji Asuka scene. More or less, Shinji tries to come onto Asuka because he doesn't really feel comfortable with anyone except her at that point, she's a last option. He's also not really looking to support, help, or understand her in any way, he just wants someone as an emotional crutch. She's rather hurt not only at this but also that his feelings for her were more or less carnal and not romantic in the fullest sense of the word. Not entirely out of character for a 14 year old boy to have this flaw, nor for a 14 year old girl to be hurt by it. Point is, Shinji had little interest in Asuka as a PERSON. Asuka wants Shinji to genuinely have interest and care about her as a person, like a friend would. We have this lyric from Wannabe: If you wanna be my lover, you gotta get with my friends (Gotta get with my friends) Make it last forever, friendship never ends Then, he's only looking for an emotional crutch offering nothing in return. He just wants to take. Hence the lyrics: If you wanna be my lover, you have got to give Taking is too easy, but that's the way it is It's impressive how much Anno's writing lines up with real feminine perspective. He's a fan of reading romance novels written by women and it shows. Follow me for more musings

Article: Spice Girls and EoE
[]: Spice Girls & EoE             Quote Post by Asunji_Yuko » Mon Feb 12, 2024 5:31 pm For inexplicable reasons, the hit 90s pop song "Wannabe" by the Spice Girls popped in my head while I was reading a 15 year old analysis about the pre-3rd instrumentality Shinji Asuka scene. More or less, Shinji tries to come onto Asuka because he doesn't really feel comfortable with anyone except her at that point, she's a last option. He's also not really looking to support, help, or understand her in any way, he just wants someone as an emotional crutch. She's rather hurt not only at this but also that his feelings for her were more or less carnal and not romantic in the fullest sense of the word. Not entirely out of character for a 14 year old boy to have this flaw, nor for a 14 year old girl to be hurt by it. Point is, Shinji had little interest in Asuka as a PERSON. Asuka wants Shinji to genuinely have interest and care about her as a person, like a friend would. We have this lyric from Wannabe: If you wanna be my lover, you gotta get with my friends (Gotta get with my friends) Make it last forever, friendship never ends Then, he's only looking for an emotional crutch offering nothing in return. He just wants to take. Hence the lyrics: If you wanna be my lover, you have got to give Taking is too easy, but that's the way it is It's impressive how much Anno's writing lines up with real feminine perspective. He's a fan of reading romance novels written by women and it shows. Follow me for more musings
